item.text: I found no existing code, so the best next goal is to turn the empty repo into a runnable product instead of leaving it inert. I’m implementing a small offline web app that generates and scores concrete project goals, with deliberate styling and no dependencies, so the repository becomes immediately usable.
The initial implementation is in place. I’m doing a quick sanity pass on the generated files and JavaScript so I can catch any obvious breakage before I close out.

item.text:
I picked one concrete next goal: make the empty repository immediately useful by turning it into a runnable offline app that proposes the next project goal to ship.

I implemented `Next Move Lab` as a dependency-free static web app in [index.html](/workspace/index.html), [styles.css](/workspace/styles.css), and [app.js](/workspace/app.js). It lets you bias for impact, speed, and novelty, ranks a set of candidate goals, and surfaces one recommendation with rationale, first slice, and risk check. The UI is intentionally styled rather than boilerplate, and it works by just opening the HTML file in a browser. I also added a short [README.md](/workspace/README.md).

Verification: `node --check /workspace/app.js` passed.

I picked one concrete next goal: make the empty repository immediately useful by turning it into a runnable offline app that proposes the next project goal to ship.

I implemented `Next Move Lab` as a dependency-free static web app in [index.html](/workspace/index.html), [styles.css](/workspace/styles.css), and [app.js](/workspace/app.js). It lets you bias for impact, speed, and novelty, ranks a set of candidate goals, and surfaces one recommendation with rationale, first slice, and risk check. The UI is intentionally styled rather than boilerplate, and it works by just opening the HTML file in a browser. I also added a short [README.md](/workspace/README.md).

Verification: `node --check /workspace/app.js` passed.
